design_philosophy.core_loop: Explore Britannia, interact with NPCs through dialogue trees, solve environmental puzzles via object manipulation, engage in real-time party-based combat, uncover and thwart conspiracies threatening the world
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Moral virtue versus deception and corruption; player agency in a world where nearly everything is interactive and consequences emerge from choice
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embodying The Avatar—a paragon of virtue navigating a richly interactive medieval fantasy world where individual agency and moral choices shape outcomes
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Ultima VII is radically opinionated about interactivity: virtually every object in the game world can be manipulated (moved, taken, used). The shift from grid/tile-based movement to continuous, mouse-driven interaction represents a philosophical stance that the game world should respond to player intent rather than constrain it. Dialogue trees gate information by narrative necessity rather than random keyword entry, enforcing story coherence. Real-time combat with AI-controlled party members prioritizes immersion and world consistency over turn-based tactical control. The game trusts player morality rather than enforcing it mechanically.

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: High: The game's real-time combat allows players to pause inventory/menu access to prepare items and spells. Party composition and AI behavior settings (e.g., aggressive vs. defensive stances) provide tactical flexibility. The open-world exploration allows players to avoid or engage threats as they choose.

progression.upgrade_system: Party equipment upgrades, spell acquisition through learning, character stat growth. Inventory management through drag-and-drop container system (gumps) affects tactical readiness.
progression.unlock_mechanism: Dialogue tree progression gates information and quests by narrative necessity; NPCs reveal keywords and options only when story-appropriate. Environmental object manipulation unlocks new paths (e.g., stacking items to create stairs).
replayability_hooks: Multiple moral choice pathways and dialogue options
Interactive world objects enable emergent solutions to problems
Party composition flexibility (up to 8 party members)

world_building_techniques: Dialogue trees with context-gated keyword discovery
Environmental storytelling through interactive object placement and use (baking bread, forging weapons, painting self-portraits, changing baby swaddling)
NPC schedules and behaviors that reflect their role in the world
Multi-layered, stackable world objects that create spatial narrative
Plot-driven mysteries and deceptions revealed through character interaction

faction_indicators: NPC allegiances to various groups (implied by conspiracy plots and crown authority undermining)
Moral virtue evaluation of the Avatar's actions
danger_signals: Plots and schemes threatening world stability
environmental_storytelling: Interactive object placement reflecting NPC daily routines and professions
Stacked objects forming environmental puzzles and barriers
World state changes in response to player actions
Multi-layered map design revealing hidden locations and story elements

sources_used: Wikipedia: Ultima VII: The Black Gate
